answer: The FeederBW dataset, recorded by the German distribution system operator Netze BW, provides real-world energy data from 200 low-voltage feeders (Treutlein et al., 2026, p. 1). This unique dataset covers two years (2023-2025) and features a one-minute temporal resolution, along with high photovoltaic feed-in data (Treutlein et al., 2026, p. 1). It includes detailed metadata, such as feeder-specific information like the number of housing units, installed power of low-carbon technology, and aggregated industrial energy data (Treutlein et al., 2026, p. 1). Additionally, the dataset incorporates weather information, which is matched to feeder measurement data and metadata using zip codes (Treutlein et al., 2026, p. 1, 6).

answer: Some participants have accused energy advisors of having commercial interests, which jeopardizes a trusting relationship (Scheller et al., 2021, p. 19). This distrust is often rooted in the assumption that energy advisors are not independent actors, with some experts even describing them as "salesmen" (Scheller et al., 2021, p. 12). Despite this, many participants expressed a desire for support to simplify information gathering and the planning process, a role that energy advisors could potentially fill by providing independent advice (Scheller et al., 2021, p. 19). Stakeholders with commercial interests, such as energy advisors, are frequently viewed with wariness (Scheller et al., 2021, p. 22).
